topic: China SG Network Null (2009 Eclipse) — change-driven re-review after d9290d0 +177% evidence rewrite
change_summary: Commit d9290d0 (2026-04-21T19:44Z) expanded detail_evidence from 1,210 → 3,347 chars (+177%) per EXP-206 companion finding: (a) replaced stale 'E-PRED-C 0.0 µGal prospective-prediction' acknowledgment with PRED-TIER-3 structural self-contradiction (10–13 µGal pulse vs 2009 null), (b) added Sun 2010 source-attribution concern (closest match is 2013 CJG doi:10.6038/cjg20130306), (c) added steel-man 'complementary not contradictory' counter-narrative paragraph with SG-sensitivity rebuttal (~1,000× vs spring gravimeter), (d) added tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields not present at c2. No verdict change.
our_argument_summary: WIN-014's core framing is preserved from c2: the dome claims both positive gravity-eclipse signals (WIN-011/012 Mohe and BOU) and null gravity results (WIN-013/014) as simultaneous confirmations, without advance specification. The c3 rewrite adds three new arguments: (1) Sun 2010 source may be unverifiable (closest candidate 2013 CJG paper doesn't match cited date); (2) monitor.py provides no computational validation for WIN-014; (3) the dome's own August 2026 prediction PRED-TIER-3 calls for a 10–13 µGal SG pulse in the very instrument class whose 2009 null the dome claims as confirmation, creating a structural self-contradiction. Verdict 'Misleading' preserved; TLDR and detail_verdict_text continue to describe 'unfalsifiable structure' rather than the stronger self-contradiction framing the new evidence text introduces.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (lines 92–93: 'Source: Sun 2010. 0.0 uGal across network. Replicates WIN-013.')
absence: no monitor.py hook in raw-text; no automated pull_data.py logic for this WIN
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No dome computation. The 2009 China SG claim is a textual citation on wins.html only. The dome's forward SG prediction (PRED-TIER-3) is stored in the predictions page, not wired into monitor.py.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Static textual claim. No pred vs obs comparison exists in the dome repository.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None in code; rhetorical escape clauses exist in the dome's 'complementary not contradictory' framing.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: raw-text/02-wins.txt shows WIN-014 as a static text entry with no domain, API, or pull_data.py hook. No 'china.*sg|2009.*eclipse|Sun.*2010' matches in any monitor.py snapshot. Tag 'none' matches current wins.json.code_analysis.monitoring.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics expects null SG signal during eclipses (no gravitational-shielding mechanism is recognized), so the dome's claim that 0.0 µGal CONFIRMS its model is arguably a relabel of the trivial-null default. But the tag is borderline: the CLAIM structure (null = confirmation via a new mechanism) is not a standard-physics prediction per se — it is a post-hoc reinterpretation of a default expectation. Keeping 'false' is defensible; a case for 'true' could be made. Current tag matches wins.json.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 2009 eclipse data pre-dates the dome model by ~15 years. The dome adopted the null as 'confirmation' rather than predicting it in advance. Tag 'true' confirmed.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The 0.0 µGal claim is not derived from dome geometry (disc radius, firmament height) — it's simply the observed value re-interpreted. Tag 'false' confirmed.

holes_found.description: Internal inconsistency with SEC-8b.3 regarding E-PRED-C's current existence on the dome site. WIN-014 detail_evidence asserts: 'The dome's current live predictions page (predictions.html, accessed 2026-04-21) contains no prediction labeled "E-PRED-C."' But SEC-8b.3 (data/sections.json part8b.html, current text) asserts the dome currently publishes E-PRED-C: 'The dome also publishes four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests (E-PRED-A peak-tracks-geometry, E-PRED-B Hartland-beats-Ebro, E-PRED-C SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal, E-PRED-D non-path stations below noise floor) for the August 2026 eclipse.' Present tense 'publishes' in SEC-8b.3 directly contradicts WIN-014's 'contains no prediction labeled E-PRED-C.' A dome defender (or even a careful neutral reader) who reads both sections will immediately notice the contradiction and will have no way to decide which of our sections is accurate. Note that raw-text/03-predictions.txt (extracted 2026-03-12) does contain 'E-PRED-C — SG gravimeters 0.0 uGal' verbatim, so our own source corpus supports SEC-8b.3 and contradicts WIN-014's strong phrasing. EXP-206 cites a WebFetch on 2026-04-21 confirming E-PRED-C absent, but no fresh raw-text dump has been committed post-WebFetch — meaning SEC-8b.3 and WIN-014 are now written against different snapshots of the dome site, and both sections cannot simultaneously be correct.
holes_found.recommendation: Reconcile via one of three paths. (a) Re-poll the dome's predictions.html and commit a fresh raw-text dump; if E-PRED-C is genuinely absent, update SEC-8b.3 to match WIN-014 (remove E-PRED-C from the sub-test list, or qualify as 'previously published, since relabeled as PRED-TIER-*'). (b) If E-PRED-C is still present, update WIN-014 to acknowledge coexistence — the dome MAY publish BOTH E-PRED-C (generic 0.0 µGal null) AND PRED-TIER-3 (contact-phase 10–13 µGal residual) as separate sub-tests of the same eclipse, which would itself be a strong self-contradiction argument. (c) At minimum, replace WIN-014's absolute 'contains no prediction labeled E-PRED-C' with 'the dome's prominently-featured August-2026 SG prediction is now PRED-TIER-3 (10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse), regardless of whether the legacy E-PRED-C label still appears on the predictions page'. Option (c) is the lowest-risk edit and preserves the structural self-contradiction argument without taking a claim we cannot fully verify.

holes_found.description: The 'would have captured' claim in the PRED-TIER-3 self-contradiction paragraph assumes 2009 Chinese SG methodology matches PRED-TIER-3's 'Level-3 residuals.' WIN-014 asserts: 'The 2009 China SG campaign — continuously sampled across C1/C4 — would have captured a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse if one existed. It saw none.' But PRED-TIER-3's actual claim (per data/predictions.json) is '10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals' — a specific signal-processing output that requires Earth-tide removal, drift correction, and environmental-noise subtraction. Whether the 2009 Chinese SG analysis (Sun 2010 or the 2013 CJG paper) produced Level-3 residuals, and whether Level-3 processing across a ~7-hour event would have isolated a ~20-minute contact-phase pulse, is not established in the WIN-014 text. A competent dome defender will say: 'PRED-TIER-3 is a Level-3 residual claim. The 2009 campaign's published analysis may not have produced Level-3 residuals at contact-phase resolution; the raw time series would be dominated by ~300 µGal Earth tides and a 10-µGal contact-phase pulse could easily be lost without the specific processing PRED-TIER-3 calls for.' That rebuttal weakens the 'whichever horn holds, the other fails' punchline.
holes_found.recommendation: Strengthen the 'would have captured' claim by citing a floor argument rather than a methodology-match assumption. Replacement: 'Superconducting gravimeters have raw noise floors below 1 µGal at minute timescales (Van Camp 2005; Crossley et al. 2013); a ~10 µGal contact-phase pulse would appear in the 2009 time series regardless of whether Level-3 residual processing was applied. Any such pulse of physical origin would have been reported at the time, not discovered retrospectively via PRED-TIER-3's 2026 methodology.' If that floor argument cannot be verified for the specific 2009 Chinese instruments, weaken the punchline to 'the dome now promises a 10–13 µGal SG signal in the instrument class whose 2009 null it originally claimed as confirmation; the two claims cannot both be directly measurable truths about SG behavior during eclipses.' The weaker wording preserves the structural point without the methodological overclaim.

holes_found.description: The Sun 2010 source-attribution concern rhetorically undermines the downstream 2009-null-as-evidence argument. New text says: 'published results in English-language journals remain elusive — the closest candidate is a 2013 Chinese Journal of Geophysics paper (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306), which does not match the 2010 date cited by the dome. If no peer-reviewed publication exists under the cited reference, the dome is relying on an unverifiable source for this WIN.' Then later, the same paragraph asserts 'The 2009 China SG campaign — continuously sampled across C1/C4 — would have captured a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse if one existed. It saw none.' These two claims are in rhetorical tension: if we successfully cast doubt on whether the 2009 campaign's published analysis is citable, we have weakened our own ability to assert what the 2009 data shows or doesn't show. A defender will say: 'Pick one. Either the 2009 data is unverified (in which case you cannot assert it saw no pulse), or the 2009 data is real (in which case stop implying Sun 2010 is a phantom citation).'
holes_found.recommendation: Resolve the tension in one of two ways. (a) Verify Sun 2010 directly: the 2013 CJG paper (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) with Chinese-language title 'Observation of the changes of solar eclipse over the gravitational field and its explanation' (Sun et al.) is very likely the English-accessible version of a Chinese Academy of Sciences result first circulated in 2010 Chinese technical literature — Chinese papers commonly reach CJG two or three years after preliminary circulation. If this resolves to a real publication chain, drop the attribution concern entirely and cite the 2013 paper directly. (b) If verification is not practical this cycle, restructure the paragraph so the source concern does not precede the 2009-data-as-dispositive argument — move attribution concerns to a footnote or late-paragraph aside, and lead with what the dome itself states about the 2009 network (the dome's own wins.txt: 'Source: Sun 2010. 0.0 uGal across network.') as the operative self-description.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN inconsistency: WIN-012 and WIN-014 cite different dome PRED-TIER-* labels as the August-2026 SG contact-phase claim. WIN-012 detail_evidence references PRED-TIER-2 (5 occurrences) — which predictions.json defines as '10–13 µGal gravity pulse at C1/C4 eclipse contacts.' WIN-014 detail_evidence references PRED-TIER-3 (exclusively) — defined as 'SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals.' Both predictions have the same magnitude (10–13 µGal), overlapping test windows (August 2026 eclipse), and similar instrument scope (SG contact-phase). A careful reader asking 'which dome prediction does the 2009 null actually refute?' cannot get a consistent answer from our review. It may be that PRED-TIER-2 and PRED-TIER-3 are effectively the same prediction under two labels (redundant bookkeeping on the dome's side) or two distinct predictions (PRED-TIER-2 = raw time-series pulse, PRED-TIER-3 = post-processed Level-3 residual). Our review should take a consistent position. This is relevant to the c3 review because the EXP-206 'companion finding' that drove the d9290d0 rewrite deliberately switched WIN-014 to PRED-TIER-3 while WIN-012 was previously patched to PRED-TIER-2 — EXP-206 did not cross-reference WIN-012 to check consistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Write a single ~2-sentence explanatory clause that covers both WINs. Candidate: 'The dome's August 2026 SG commitment appears in two partially-overlapping prediction records — PRED-TIER-2 (C1/C4 contact-phase pulse in raw time series) and PRED-TIER-3 (Level-3 residual variant). Both call for a 10–13 µGal signal in the instrument class the dome's 2009 null treats as dispositive of a globe-only mechanism.' Then align WIN-012 and WIN-014 to each cite 'PRED-TIER-2/3' or 'the PRED-TIER-2/-3 SG commitment' rather than one or the other singly. Close the remaining analyst task: determine whether the dome's live predictions.html treats these as one prediction or two, and file a follow-up patch if they are genuinely separate.

holes_found.description: The 'verdict' vs 'verdict text' vs 'new evidence' now describe three different framings of WIN-014 and do not align. verdict = 'Misleading'. detail_verdict_text = 'Same unfalsifiable structure as WIN-013: the model claims credit for opposite outcomes across different experiments without advance specification of expected results.' tldr_verdict = 'Same unfalsifiable structure as WIN-013 — claiming opposite outcomes confirms the model either way.' But the NEW paragraph added in d9290d0 closes with: 'The dome cannot simultaneously claim (a) that SG nulls confirm the model (WIN-014, 2009) and (b) that SG gravimeters will show a 10–13 µGal pulse at C1/C4 in Aug 2026 (PRED-TIER-3). Whichever horn holds, the other fails.' That closing is a self-contradiction claim (WIN-067 verdict pattern), not an unfalsifiability claim. The WIN-014 body now makes a stronger argument than its verdict advertises. Either the verdict/TLDRs should be upgraded to 'Self-Contradicted' (matching the closing paragraph), OR the closing paragraph should be softened to frame the PRED-TIER-3 commitment as 'further evidence of the unfalsifiable structure' rather than as a standalone self-contradiction. Leaving the current mismatch means readers who stop at the verdict get one argument; readers who finish the evidence get a different (stronger) one.
holes_found.recommendation: Upgrade verdict to 'Self-Contradicted' and rewrite detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict to match. The structural self-contradiction (WIN-014 null + PRED-TIER-3 positive, both dome-authored, both about SG-class instruments) is the c3 argument's strongest point and should be the verdict, not buried in the closing paragraph. Proposed detail_verdict_text: 'Self-contradicted by the dome's own August 2026 prediction. The dome claims the 2009 SG null confirms its model; it also registers PRED-TIER-2/-3 calling for a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse in the same instrument class. Whichever outcome obtains in August 2026, one of the two dome commitments fails.' Proposed tldr_verdict: 'Self-contradicted by the dome's own August 2026 SG prediction (10–13 µGal pulse). The dome cannot call both the 2009 null and a positive 2026 pulse confirmations.'

holes_found.description: The '~1,000× sensitivity' claim is imported from WIN-013's Van Camp et al. 2001 comparison (four European SGs vs Mohe spring gravimeter). It is re-asserted here as relevant to the 2009 Chinese SG network, but Chinese SG network specs are not cited. The ratio may be broadly correct (SG sensitivity is typically ~0.1 µGal vs spring ~100 µGal yielding ~1,000×), but citing a WIN-013-era European comparison as authority for 2009 Chinese instruments is a small rhetorical overreach. A defender could say: 'your ~1,000× ratio is for 1999-era European SGs; the 2009 Chinese network may be different hardware with different noise characteristics, and you have not established the floor for that specific campaign.'
holes_found.recommendation: Either cite a 2009-era Chinese SG specification (e.g., the Wuhan SG or the National Center for Ocean Standards SG that typically participate in Chinese eclipse networks typically show ~0.1–1 µGal noise floors at minute timescales) or weaken to 'superconducting gravimeters of this generation typically show noise floors ~100–1,000× below spring gravimeters, so a ~10 µGal signal would be conspicuous in the 2009 time series even without sensitivity-matched analysis.' The weaker phrasing survives without a specific citation.

holes_found.description: The 'physically meaningless' phrasing overstates. Current text: 'A spring gravimeter and a superconducting gravimeter measure the same physical quantity; the distinction between electromagnetic and gravitational mechanisms for a gravimeter measurement is physically meaningless.' This is mostly right — both respond to vertical acceleration — but a defender with instrument-physics background could point out that SG test masses are magnetically levitated superconducting spheres, and the system has (shielded but not infinite) susceptibility to external magnetic-field change; the distinction between 'gravitational acceleration' and 'spurious EM-induced test-mass motion' is not meaningless for an SG in principle, even if shielding and calibration reduce it to negligible in practice. Overstating with 'physically meaningless' gives the defender an easy technical quibble.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften to 'the dome's own WIN-011 positive-signal claim is based on Mohe spring-gravimeter data; if a −6.5 µGal coupling-mediated signal exists, both instrument classes should see it at their respective noise floors. The instruments differ in susceptibility to non-gravitational noise, but not in what quantity they measure.' This retains the argumentative force while conceding the technical nuance.

holes_found.description: Statistical provenance of 'Eight gravimeters were deployed across six sites in China for this eclipse' is uncited. This fact appears to originate from the 2013 CJG paper (or possibly a Chinese Academy of Sciences technical report), but no attribution is given. Given the surrounding text is simultaneously casting doubt on the cited source (Sun 2010), floating the specific deployment numbers without attribution invites the question 'where does this count come from if you cannot verify Sun 2010?'
holes_found.recommendation: Add a parenthetical citation: '(eight gravimeters across six sites per the 2013 CJG summary paper, doi:10.6038/cjg20130306)', OR remove the specific count and simply say 'multiple gravimeters deployed across Chinese eclipse-path sites'. The specific count isn't load-bearing; only the 'continuously sampled across C1/C4' property is.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own wins.txt (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 92) directly describes WIN-014 as 'China SG Network Null (2009 Eclipse) CONFIRMED / Source: Sun 2010. 0.0 uGal across network. Replicates WIN-013.' The word 'Replicates' is a concession: the dome ITSELF treats WIN-013 and WIN-014 as the same evidence type (two SG nulls). If the dome claims WIN-013 and WIN-014 are replicates, and PRED-TIER-2/-3 claims a POSITIVE SG pulse in the same instrument class at the next eclipse (2026), then by the dome's own replication logic either WIN-013 + WIN-014 should be Un-confirmed by PRED-TIER-2/-3 outcome, or PRED-TIER-2/-3 are overcommitments the dome will retreat from. The 'Replicates WIN-013' self-description is a cleaner structural contradiction than the independently-derived Chapman/κ analysis and requires no external calibration.

The κ-kinematic self-contradiction already written into WIN-012's c5 closing paragraph (per EXP-206 'Closing beat now cites PRED-TIER-2 calling for a −10 to −13 µGal downward pulse at C1/C4... at κ = 1.67 nT/µGal, ΔB/κ yields −10.2 to −12.6 µGal — consistent with PRED-TIER-2 but incompatible with the Mohe datum that anchors κ itself') is relevant here and is not cross-referenced. WIN-014 could gain a one-sentence link: 'The same κ-kinematic inconsistency flagged at WIN-012 applies here — the dome uses the Mohe −6.5 µGal datum to fit κ, then predicts −10 to −13 µGal at the next eclipse, while treating the intervening 2009 null as a confirming replicate. Three outcomes (−6.5, 0.0, and −10/−13) in the same instrument class across three eclipses cannot all be dome predictions.'

kernel_of_truth.description: The d9290d0 rewrite materially strengthens WIN-014: (a) the 'E-PRED-C phantom label' citation-integrity fix is the same class of correction that closed WIN-012 H1 and is genuinely warranted if the WebFetch evidence holds; (b) the PRED-TIER-3 self-contradiction argument is strictly stronger than the c2 'unfalsifiable structure' framing, because it grounds the dome's own forward prediction against its own backward claim rather than relying on external logic; (c) the monitor.py 'no computational support' observation is correctly tagged as 'none' and strengthens the WIN-014-as-static-citation critique. These three gains justify the +177% evidence expansion.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The gains come bundled with three new load-bearing assertions (E-PRED-C is absent from current live page; Level-3 residual processing would have captured the 2009 pulse; Sun 2010 may be unverifiable) that a competent defender can isolate and attack in series. Each is individually rebuttable, and the assertions partially undermine each other (Hole 3). A reader who compares WIN-014 to SEC-8b.3 finds an outright contradiction on E-PRED-C's current status. The c3 version advances the argument but ships with visible seams; the c4 patch work is an internal-consistency pass plus one optional Level-3 floor citation.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome-sympathetic reviewer who has accepted our methodological principles could mount the following rebuttal: (1) Our own SEC-8b.3 acknowledges E-PRED-A, -B, -C, and -D as currently-published sub-tests of the August 2026 eclipse; WIN-014's claim that E-PRED-C has been removed from the live site is therefore either stale or self-inconsistent with our own synchronic reading of the dome's materials. (2) The dome MAY publish BOTH E-PRED-C (null for non-path / non-contact phases) AND PRED-TIER-3 (10–13 µGal for contact phases specifically) — both can be true simultaneously if PRED-TIER-3 targets a narrow sub-minute C1/C4 window while E-PRED-C targets the bulk of the ~7-hour event. That would not be a self-contradiction on the dome's part; it would be a two-clause prediction. Our review reads them as contradictory because we treat 'SG 0.0 µGal' and 'SG 10–13 µGal' as mutually exclusive, but they address different time windows of the same event. (3) The 2009 Chinese SG network's published analysis (Sun 2010 / CJG 2013) did not apply Level-3 residual processing at contact-phase temporal resolution; the 2009 null reflects the null at the time-averaging resolution applied, not a null at the ~minute-scale where PRED-TIER-3 operates. So WIN-014's 'would have captured' claim is not established. (4) Our own source-attribution concern (Sun 2010 unverified) undermines our assertion that the 2009 data shows a null at all. If Sun 2010 is citable, our source concern is wrong; if Sun 2010 is not citable, the 2009 null itself is not citable and cannot be used as WIN-014 evidence.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: The defense rates 4/5 because it requires text changes at two load-bearing points (E-PRED-C absence claim, Level-3 residual methodology match). Preemption: (a) Reconcile E-PRED-C across WIN-014 and SEC-8b.3 (Hole 1 recommendation); (b) Either cite SG noise floor independent of Level-3 processing (Hole 2 recommendation) or weaken to 'the dome now promises a positive SG signal at contact phase in the instrument class its own 2009 null treats as confirmation; the two commitments cannot both be measurable truths about the same instrument class at the same event'; (c) Either verify Sun 2010 (Hole 3 recommendation (a)) or move the source concern to a late-paragraph aside that doesn't rhetorically precede the load-bearing 2009-data claims.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-012 detail_evidence cites PRED-TIER-2 (5 occurrences) as the dome's forward SG commitment; WIN-014 detail_evidence cites PRED-TIER-3 exclusively. Both are about 10–13 µGal SG contact-phase signals at the August 2026 eclipse. Our review does not reconcile whether these are the same dome prediction under two labels (redundant bookkeeping) or two genuinely distinct predictions. A defender compiling our statements will find: WIN-012 + WIN-014 + SEC-8b.3 (which references E-PRED-C, -D, etc.) together produce a 'our review has three different names for the dome's SG prediction, and does not say how they relate' critique. WIN-013 (Membach SG Null 1999) uses the same null-as-confirmation structure WIN-014 does, and both sections should probably carry the same structural self-contradiction argument against PRED-TIER-2/-3 — currently WIN-013 does not (it was not included in the d9290d0 patch).

claims_checked: SG sensitivity ratio ~1,000× vs Mohe spring gravimeter
Fingerprint field lengths (claim=13, finding=37, detail_evidence=3347, detail_verdict_text=166, tldr_evidence=239, tldr_verdict=99)
quantitative_verification.computations: Fingerprint deltas c2→c3: claim 13→13 (+0%), finding 37→37 (+0%), detail_evidence 1210→3347 (+176.6%), detail_verdict 166→166 (+0%), tldr fields added (c2 had none). verdict unchanged. All non-zero-change fields verified by direct read of clone data/wins.json post git pull --rebase. SG sensitivity ratio (~1,000×) not independently verified against 2009 Chinese SG network specs; see Hole 6. No numerical WIN-014 claims are internal to the dome (0.0 µGal and 10–13 µGal are the dome's own numbers).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: The ~1,000× sensitivity ratio is imported from WIN-013 context and not individually verified for 2009 Chinese SG hardware. Minor hole filed as Hole 6.

deeper_analysis: The d9290d0 rewrite (EXP-206 companion finding) is a genuine improvement but inherits a latent contradiction with SEC-8b.3 because the two sections were patched against different snapshots of the dome's predictions page. SEC-8b.3 was last rewritten in EXP-195 on 2026-04-21 morning using raw-text/03-predictions.txt (2026-03-12 snapshot) where E-PRED-A/-B/-C/-D are explicit. WIN-014 was patched ~12h later using a WebFetch (2026-04-21 afternoon) where E-PRED-C is allegedly absent. Without a re-polled raw-text dump capturing the post-replacement state, WIN-014 now asserts something SEC-8b.3 denies — and a reader comparing the two sections has no principled reason to trust one over the other. This is the #1 hole; it is trivially fixable by running the poller and committing a fresh raw-text snapshot, then reconciling both sections against that snapshot. The second- and third-tier holes (Level-3 methodology, Sun 2010 attribution rhetoric) are subtler but independently rebuttable. Upgrading the verdict to 'Self-Contradicted' is the single highest-leverage edit because it aligns the verdict with the strongest argument in the body and parallels the WIN-067 'Self-Contradicted' pattern (another dome-internal self-contradiction WIN). Per the 'other side of the argument' check: a dome defender will NOT accept the structural self-contradiction if any of the three major-hole rebuttals land (E-PRED-C absence, Level-3 capture, or Sun 2010 verifiability), so the upgrade to 'Self-Contradicted' should be conditioned on closing at least Hole 1 (E-PRED-C reconciliation) and Hole 2 (Level-3 or floor-based 'would have captured' argument). Hole 3 (Sun 2010) is more about rhetorical hygiene than argumentative load-bearing.

citations_verified: raw-text/02-wins.txt line 92–93: 'WIN-014 — China SG Network Null (2009 Eclipse) CONFIRMED / Source: Sun 2010. 0.0 uGal across network. Replicates WIN-013.' — verbatim match to our characterization of the dome's claim
raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 39: 'E-PRED-C — SG gravimeters 0.0 uGal' — present in 2026-03-12 snapshot, directly contradicting WIN-014's 'contains no prediction labeled E-PRED-C' assertion
data/predictions.json PRED-TIER-3 claim 'SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals' verified
data/predictions.json PRED-TIER-2 claim '10–13 µGal gravity pulse at C1/C4 eclipse contacts' verified

data/sections.json part8b.html references 'E-PRED-A peak-tracks-geometry, E-PRED-B Hartland-beats-Ebro, E-PRED-C SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal, E-PRED-D non-path stations below noise floor' — confirming SEC-8b.3 / WIN-014 internal contradiction
data/wins.json WIN-014 code_analysis tag set (monitoring:'none', post_hoc:true, derives_from_dome:false, relabels_standard:false) matches the monitoring absence observed in available raw-text

EXP-206 (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-206.json) companion_finding explicitly claims WebFetch 2026-04-21 confirms E-PRED-C absence; however, no fresh raw-text snapshot was committed to substantiate this in the canonical data corpus
citations_failed: 'Sun et al. 2010' — no verified English-language publication under exactly this citation; closest candidate 2013 Chinese Journal of Geophysics paper (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) with a 3-year date mismatch. Unresolved in the text

'Superconducting gravimeters have ~1,000× the sensitivity of the Mohe spring gravimeter' — generic SG vs spring sensitivity comparison is broadly correct but not specifically verified for the 2009 Chinese SG network hardware

'The 2009 China SG campaign — continuously sampled across C1/C4 — would have captured a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse' — the 'continuously sampled' characterization is plausible (IGETS-style networks sample at 1 Hz or higher) but the 'would have captured' inference requires Level-3 residual processing or a noise-floor floor argument that is not supplied in-section
citations_unchecked: 2013 Chinese Journal of Geophysics paper (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) — abstract/text not retrieved; may or may not describe the 2009 Chinese SG eclipse campaign under the Sun 2010 authorship chain
Van Camp et al. (2001) comparative SG sensitivity paper — re-cited from WIN-013 context, not re-verified for this review
